title: oenothera tetragona subsp. glauca
intro: Oenothera tetragona subsp. glauca is a subspecies of the evening primrose plant, characterized by its silvery-gray leaves and delicate white flowers. Traditionally, Oenothera tetragona subsp. glauca has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and antioxidant effects, which may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Oenothera tetragona subsp. glauca can be prepared as a salve, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote skin health and tissue repair.
